titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Norman Rubington Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11067281.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 04:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Norman Rubington Papers contain the writings, films, and collages of Norman Rubington.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 7-14 (motion picture film): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Norman Rubington Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Rubington Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The collection received preliminary processing at the time of acquisition. Further organization, rehousing, and description were carried out in 2012.

The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: Uncat MSS 81, 97, 98, 112, 130, 134, 175, and 183. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during basic processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Norman Rubington
bioghist.p: Norman Rubington, American author, painter, poet, filmmaker, and collagist. He published several novels under the name Akbar Del Piombo. Rubington was born in New Haven, Connecticut in 1921 and died in New York City in January 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Norman Rubington Papers contain the writings, films, and collages of Norman Rubington.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight groupings: I. November 2000 Acquisition. II. January 2001 Acquisition. III. January 2001 Acquisition. IV. February 2001 Acquisition. V. April 2001 Acquisition. VI. May 2001 Acquisition. VII. July 2001 Acquisition. VIII. May 2001 Acquisition.

did.unittitle: "New York via Pluto" by Akbar del Piombo
Is God French?, typescript drafts, corrected
The End of Affection, typescript screenplay, mimeograph corrected, by Rubington a.k.a. Akbar del Piombo
Demi-tasse: The Intimate Coffee Table Book: An Investigation into the Life and Times of Akbar del Piombo, printed version
Is God French?, collage novel, typescript, photocopy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescript drafts, corrected, of Rubington's writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: Writings and Other Papers.

did.unittitle: Soup du Jour II: Orient, typescript drafts, corrected
Much to Say Who to One Another
Orpheus in the Real World/ Jarvis
When Poets Die the Last Day
The End of Affection, typescript screenplay, corrected, by Rubington a.k.a. Akbar del Piombo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescript drafts, corrected, of Rubington's writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains films by Rubington. Box 15 contains the original film cans. Films have been rehoused and placed in cold storage.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 7-14 (motion picture film): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
